chapter_title.en: Reward of reciting Surah Juma, Munafiqun and Aala

text: : الْوَاجِبُ عَلَی کُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ إِذَا کَانَ لَنَا شِیعَهً أَنْ یَقْرَأَ فِی لَیْلَهِ الْجُمُعَهِ بِالْجُمُعَهِ وَ سَبِّحِ اسْمَ رَبِّکَ الْأَعْلَی وَ فِی صَلَاهِ الظُّهْرِ بِالْجُمُعَهِ وَ الْمُنَافِقِینَ فَإِذَا فَعَلَ ذَلِکَ فَکَأَنَّمَا یَعْمَلُ کَعَمَلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص وَ کَانَ جَزَاؤُهُ وَ ثَوَابُهُ عَلَی اللَّهِ الْجَنَّهَ.

en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs: 1. From the previous chain from al-Hassan from Sayf b. ’Umayra from Mansur b. Hazim from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “It is obligatory on every believer who is our Shia to recite Surah Juma and Surah Aala every Friday eve, and recite Surah Juma and Munafiqun in Friday noon prayer. A person doing so is like a person who is performing the deed of the Messenger of Allah (sw). The reward of this deed is that Allah would grant him Paradise.”
